sources.label: Texas Instruments to acquire Silicon Labs for $231.00/share cash (~$7.5B EV), announced Feb 4 2026, close expected 1H 2027 - TI press release
Silicon Labs merger proxy (DEFM14A) - $231/share all-cash merger with Texas Instruments, special stockholder meeting April 30 2026

verdicts.rankRationale: Reiterates yesterday's remove call: SLAB is being acquired by Texas Instruments for $231/share in cash (shareholder-approved, close expected 1H 2027), so upside is structurally capped at a ~6% arb spread - there is no residual AI-node bottleneck alpha to rank. No new news since June 6 and today's -0.2% is noise, so the small negative delta just continues the de-ranking toward removal.
verdicts.bull: Deal closes on schedule with regulatory clearance, delivering the remaining ~6% spread to $231 - but that is merger-arb return, not AI-supply-chain alpha.
verdicts.neutral: Stock trades as a deal-spread instrument pegged near $231 less time value and regulatory risk; its edge/IoT silicon exposure is now irrelevant to this list's bottleneck thesis.
verdicts.bear: A regulatory block (e.g., China SAMR) breaks the deal and the stock falls back toward fundamental IoT-cycle value well below $218, after a 65% YTD run leaves no cushion.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Regulatory approvals / deal closing milestones for the TXN acquisition.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Deal break on antitrust review, removing the $231 price floor.

verdicts.rankRationale: On a CPO-alpha lens SLAB was already marginal (IoT/edge recovery, not co-packaged optics), and the pending all-cash TXN takeout at $231 caps any asymmetric upside entirely; reiterating yesterday's removal direction since the name still sits at rank 97. No genuinely new information since June 6 - the delta is small and disciplinary, not news-driven.
verdicts.bull: Arb investors collect the ~6% spread to $231 if the deal closes in 1H 2027 as guided; zero CPO optionality either way.
verdicts.neutral: A cash-capped acquisition target has no place on an asymmetric-upside CPO alpha list regardless of IoT fundamentals; it is dead weight at rank 97.
verdicts.bear: Deal break risk plus a 65% YTD run-up means the downside scenario is a sharp gap lower with no offsetting CPO thesis to fall back on.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: TXN deal regulatory clearances and closing timeline.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Antitrust/SAMR deal break collapsing the arb floor.
